title: Remove unnecessary crun check
body:
#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

```release-note
None
```

Closes #27115 by removing the skip in `run_test.go`.

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **NOT APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *givensuman* **Once this PR has been reviewed and has the lgtm label**, please assign [lsm5](https://github.com/lsm5) for approval. Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - **[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)** Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

@givensuman Thanks. Change LGTM as such. There is a much bigger diff than what's required for the issue resolution, but no biggie.

You'll need to signoff on your commit and repush to get the DCO check to pass. See the DCO description and instructions at https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/27143/checks?check_run_id=50959660798 to get the full picture.
Fixed, thanks for the help. The other changes are `gofmt` running automatically on save, I figured they were better to include than undo.
New changes are detected. LGTM label has been removed.
@givensuman The failing CI looks flaky. In your commit message, please describe what was actually changed and add `Fixes: #27115`.
I was going for some low-hanging fruit in Issues re: a test suite, I don't think it justifies failing CI though.
@givensuman I think it was perfectly fine. The only problem is that the CI is sometimes flaky, so you have to rerun the tests.
